title: Misleading choice name suggesting validation-only operation
description: A choice name suggests it performs only validation (e.g., 'Validate' or 'Check'), but the choice actually performs state-changing operations beyond validation. This can mislead developers and auditors into treating the choice as side-effect-free when it is not.
recommendation: Rename the choice to accurately reflect its actual behavior, distinguishing between validation-only and state-modifying operations.

oracle.description: A choice named to suggest validation-only behavior actually performs state mutations (create/archive/exercise).
oracle.static_signal: Choice with name containing 'Validate', 'Check', or 'Verify' that contains create/archive/exercise calls in its body

notes: Low benchmark value but detectable by static analysis: check choice names against their actual side effects.
